title: Housing Search Assistance * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Housing Search Assistance * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues 5 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Issues Domestic Violence Issues Former Foster Children Foster Children Hispanic/Latino Community Homeless Families Homeless Youth Homelessness Issues Teen Pregnancy Issues Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH COVO LOW-INCOME HOUSING FOR VETERANS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Housing units (a mix of single family homes and multi-family housing located in Bend, Oregon) are prioritized for homeless, low-income veterans. COVO also assists homeless, low-income veterans with housing search for rentals throughout Central, Oregon. 61510 S Highway 97 Suite 100 Bend, OR 977023706 Eligibility: Homeless Veterans who income-qualify and pass property management screening for tenants Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 383-2793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION RESOURCE ENTERPRISES INC HOMELESS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides case management, transitional housing, rapid re-housing, permanent supportive housing, and veteran's housing services. Offers personal grooming items and shower vouchers when available. 2310 1st Street Suite 2 Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness or at risk of Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 842-5261 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FORT KENNEDY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, pet food, clothing, showers, blood pressure checks, bus passes, legal information services, and benefit and housing resources. 7600 SE Johnson Creek Boulevard Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans, all discharge types Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm. Various programs may have specific times. Email (503) 765-2661 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to veterans in accessing VA and community services. Services may include short-term case management, onsite primary care, access to medical, mental health, and substance abuse treatment, and access to VA housing programs, including Grant and Per Diem program, HUD/VASH, Veterans Recovery House and Supportive Service for Veteran Families (SSVF) if found eligible. These grants do not provide immediate assistance, are sometimes one-time, and may include case management. Help with finding market rate or subsidized housing or linkage to community housing programs. Assistance with expediting VA Benefit claims and access to employment programs such as Supported Employment, Veterans Reintegration program, and State employment. Access to computers, clothing, and hygiene items is available onsite. 308 SW 1st Avenue Suite 155 Portland, OR 972043432 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless. Veterans must be present in the household and must be the one to request services. Some services available for Veterans who are not eligible for full VA Healthcare benefits. Hours: -Business hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 7:30am-4:30pm, Thursday 10am-4:30pm, closed federal holidays -Walk-in hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-3pm, Thursday 10am-3pm. No walk-ins after 2:30pm, better to go earlier in the day. Closed federal holidays. (503) 808-1256 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VETERANS STAND DOWNS AND VETERANS EVENTS STAND DOWN EVENTS VETERANS STAND DOWN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an annual health and wellness resource event. Services may include health screening, medical and dental care, vision, housing resources, counseling and substance abuse referrals, haircuts, pet care, job fairs, help accessing VA services and benefits, DMV and legal assistance. Meals, clothing, and hygiene supplies may also be offered. Eligibility: Veterans and military personnel; focus is generally on homeless and at-risk veterans Hours: Varies by event Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH COVO LOW-INCOME HOUSING FOR VETERANS COMMUNITY ACTION RESOURCE ENTERPRISES INC HOMELESS SERVICES FORT KENNEDY FORT KENNEDY VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER
